marked_wedges_area_from_total: Count the equal center wedges in the whole polygon and the shaded wedges. Using the total polygon area, what is the area of "{target_name}"?
Use the equal wedge decomposition: count all center wedges, then count the shaded wedges. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
The total polygon area is labeled and one or more adjacent wedges are shaded. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
Based on the equal center wedges and the shaded wedge count, what is the area of "{target_name}"?
Find the area of "{target_name}" from the visible equal-wedge decomposition and total polygon area.
wedge_area_from_side_and_apothem: Use the labeled side length and apothem of the shaded wedge. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
The shaded wedge has its base and apothem labeled. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
Find the triangular wedge area from the visible side and apothem labels. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
Based on the side length and apothem, what is the area of "{target_name}"?
Use the marked regular-polygon wedge. What is the area of "{target_name}"?
marked_wedges_central_angle: Count the equal center wedges in the whole polygon and the shaded wedge or wedges between OA and OB. What is the measure of angle {target_name} in degrees?
Use the equal center wedges of the regular polygon: count all wedges, then count the shaded wedge or wedges forming angle {target_name}. What is its measure in degrees?
The marked central angle {target_name} spans the shaded wedge or wedges. What is its measure in degrees?
Based on the regular-polygon decomposition and the shaded wedge count, find the degree measure of angle {target_name}.
What is the measure of the marked central angle {target_name} formed by the shaded wedge or wedges?
perimeter_from_total_area_and_apothem: Use the labeled total area and apothem of the regular polygon. What is the total perimeter of "{target_name}"?
The total area and apothem are labeled. What is the total perimeter of "{target_name}"?
Find the total perimeter of "{target_name}" from the visible area and apothem labels.
Based on the labeled area and apothem, what is the total perimeter of "{target_name}"?
What is the total perimeter of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_perimeter: Use the labeled perimeter of the regular polygon. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
The regular polygon perimeter is labeled. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the side length of "{target_name}" from the visible perimeter label.
Based on the labeled perimeter, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_total_area_and_apothem: Use the labeled total area and apothem of the regular polygon. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
The total area and apothem are labeled. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the side length of "{target_name}" from the visible area and apothem labels.
Based on the labeled total area and apothem, what is the length of "{target_name}"?
side_length_from_wedge_area_and_apothem: Use the labeled shaded wedge area and apothem. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
The shaded wedge area and apothem are labeled. What is the length of "{target_name}"?
Find the side length of "{target_name}" from the visible wedge-area and apothem labels.
Based on the shaded wedge and apothem, what is the length of "{target_name}"?

scene:regular_polygon_decomposition_scen.object_description: a regular polygon partitioned into equal center wedges with visible labels and measurements

global.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON object whose keys are the visible point labels shown in the example; each value must be the pixel point [x,y] at that labeled point
global.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with exactly two keys: "annotation" and "answer".
global.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with exactly one key: "answer".

task:marked_piece_area_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested area as a number rounded to one decimal place

task:wedge_area_from_side_apothem_value_.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested area as a number rounded to one decimal place

task:central_angle_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested angle in degrees as an integer

task:perimeter_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested perimeter as an integer

task:side_length_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested length as an integer
